text.title: spatial-design-tools Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the spatial-design-tools methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Tool stack MUST be declared per phase: concept (Figma + ShapesXR), prototype (Unity OR Unreal), production (same engine + USD or glTF asset pipeline). Mixing prototype tools across team in the same phase is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Phase mixing produces re-export cost and decision paralysis; one tool per phase keeps cost predictable.

rule.statement: Asset format MUST be USD/USDZ when targeting visionOS; glTF 2.0 with KTX2 textures when targeting Quest, WebXR, or Android XR. Mixed formats require a documented conversion pipeline.

rationale.source: Apple visionOS HIG; Khronos glTF spec

rule.rationale: visionOS RealityKit is USD-native; everything else consumes glTF more cheaply. Cross-format conversion needs explicit handling.

rule.statement: Polygon-count, file-size, and draw-call budgets MUST be set in concept stage, before any production asset is authored.

rationale.source: Unity Profiler docs; Meta Performance Guidelines

rule.rationale: Budgets set post-production cause rework with 5-20x the cost vs front-loading.

rule.statement: Engine choice (Unity vs Unreal) MUST be justified per project, citing team skill, platform reach, and licensing cost. Defaulting to one without justification is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Engine choice locks in skill investment for years; treat as a decision-record, not a default.

rule.statement: Playable prototype MUST be in-engine on target hardware before production starts. Screenshot or Figma-frame approvals are not sufficient.
rule.rationale: Spatial feel cannot be evaluated from flat screenshots; on-hardware playable is the only valid signal.
